results.title: Cast us not out: a film about old age
results.creator_credit: Richard Bigham, Richard Crewdson, Jewish Welfare Board and Samaritan Films
results.brief_description: Examines the plight of the old in our society, showing the work that is being done by such organizations as the Jewish Welfare Board to alleviate the problem. Experts present the harsh facts of aging and stress the unwillingness of society to confront them. Considers some of the problems of institutional care.

results.title: A Case study in mechanism (TEFC)
results.creator_credit: David Jackson, BBC-TV and Open University
results.brief_description: An examination of the benzyne mechanism for nucleophilic aromatic substitution. Queries whether SN1 or SN2 mechanisms for nucleophilic substitution at a saturated carbon atom can be applied at an unsaturated or aromatic carbon atom. Looks at evidence for a modified version of the SN2 mechanism.

results.title: The Case of the missing magnets
results.brief_description: Reviews the basic principles of human relations, and the problem of motivating employees to better teamwork, and productivity. A dramatization helps supervisors understand that discipline goes hand-in-hand with human relations in moulding an effective team.

results.title: The Case of Barbara Parsons
results.creator_credit: Morten Parker, Rob Iveson, Labor Canada and Parker Film Associates
results.brief_description: Barbara Parsons, a recent employee, is promoted to a position ahead of Michael Blais a company employee of 19 years. Dramatizes a situation that could occur in any unionized workplace - the violation of a contract between management and labour and illustrates the four main steps of the grievance procedure.

results.title: A Case for shiftwork?
results.creator_credit: Anvil Films and British Productivity Council
results.brief_description: The case for and against shiftwork in industry. The film follows the investigation of a textile factory by a visiting consultant who produces a prima facie case for shiftwork while flashbacks show it was successfully introduced in other factories. Discussion with management and shop floor illuminate various systems of shiftwork and the organizational and human problems involved in its application.

results.title: A Case for handling
results.creator_credit: Peter M Plaskitt, Humphrey Swingler and Greenpark Productions
results.brief_description: Under the guidance of a consultant, a foundry adopts numerous materials-handling devices; as a result of speeding the flow of castings from one point to another and using now redundant labourers on productive semi-skilled tasks, output increased by 20%. Although mainly concerned with the re-conversion of one factory, the film shows a wide range of materials-handling methods in other industries, including the use of cranes, lifting blocks, runways, conveyors, unit loads, palletization etc.

results.title: The Case against rubella
results.brief_description: Shows rubella-damaged children with various handicaps, including blindness and brain damage. Outlines the history of the disease and stresses that Rubella and its serious consequences can now be prevented by newly developed vaccines. Briefly shows research efforts and how the vaccines are to be used

results.title: Carve her name with pride [study extract]
results.creator_credit: Lewis Gilbert, Daniel M. Angel, Rank Organisation Film Productions and Society for Education in Film and Television
results.brief_description: An extract from “Carve her name with pride”. Tells the story of Violette Szabo, the British secret agent, who undertook various missions in Occupied France during the Second World War, until she was captured and finally shot in Germany. Cast: Violette Szabo - Virginia McKenna Jacques - Maurice Ronet Interrogator - Noel Willman

results.title: Carthage rediscovered
results.brief_description: Carthage was once a maritime city open to the whole of the ancient world, today it is a suburb of Tunis but one has only to dig beneath the asphalt of any part of modern Carthage, to find extensive remains of the ancient city. Several missions are today making excavations in various areas in the hope of saving the stones and the mosaics.

results.title: The Cars in your life
results.creator_credit: Terence Macartney-Filgate, Roman Kroitor, Tom Daly, Wolf Koenig and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: A light, humorous look at the motor car and the great North American itch for a place on the road. From the comparative peace of Honest Joe’s used-car lot, this film hurries you onto our public speedways, where hot rubber erases any distance between all points. Slow-motion and pop-on-pop photography make this a provocative, revealing study of motormania unlimited.
